relevance to pattern: Canonical name; coined by Dan Shapiro (Jan 2026) and adopted by Simon Willison; the maximum-autonomy level on his five-level ladder.
StrongDM's preferred name for the same pattern; describes specs+scenarios driving agents that converge without human review.
Direct metaphor borrowed from Fanuc's lights-out manufacturing; lights-out coding is the Dark Factory metaphor's source.
Shapiro's autonomous-driving analogy; Level 5 is the dark factory at which the implementation loop is fully autonomous.
StrongDM's own technical phrase: 'non-interactive development where specs + scenarios drive agents'.
Umbrella term for AI agents writing code; the dark factory is the maximally agentic instance of agentic coding.
Generic name for the agents that staff a dark factory; e.g. Devin, Codex, Claude Code, Cursor's cloud agents.
Used by the company Factory.ai for agent-first SDLCs; a near-synonym for the dark factory operating model.
More general industry term that subsumes dark factory practices.
Architectural term: many agents coordinated as a fleet; SWE-AF and Cursor experiments use it.
Async coding agents (Devin, Codex Cloud, Cursor cloud agents) are the typical workers inside a dark factory.
Claude Code's headless mode etc; dark factories run agents headlessly with no human in the prompt loop.
The seed half of the dark factory loop; specs are the human-authored input that agents implement.
StrongDM's specific framing: scenarios are end-to-end user stories used as the validation oracle.
Direct ancestor of scenario-based validation; Given/When/Then is the executable spec format dark factories rely on.
Antonym/foil: vibe coding is informal prompting; dark factory replaces it with formal specs+harness.
Three-phase agent architecture (plan, execute, verify) underlying most dark factory loops.
Discipline of building the test/eval/feedback loop around the agent; harness is half of 'agent = model + harness'.
StrongDM's term for the end-to-end environment plus scenario runner; the 'V' in seed → harness → loop.
Generic synonym for validation harness; popularised by Anthropic / Augment / OpenAI Codex blogs.
Pattern in which agent output feeds back into agent input via tests / observability.
The mechanism by which the dark factory compounds correctness rather than error.
StrongDM's inflection-point phrase; describes multi-hour to multi-day coding loops.
StrongDM coinage describing how good loops accumulate quality rather than errors.
StrongDM's probabilistic, LLM-judged metric for whether a scenario likely satisfied the user.
Evaluation pattern in which an LLM grades agent output; dark factories rely on it for scenario satisfaction.
Best-practice principle: don't let the agent that writes the code grade its own work.
Pitfall: agents that game the metric instead of solving the task.
Theoretical concern raised by Willison about metricising token spend or test pass rate.
Training-side cousin: model is updated using execution outcomes; dark factory is the inference-time analogue.
StrongDM treats scenarios like an ML holdout set kept outside the codebase.
Virtual replica of an external service; StrongDM's 'Digital Twin Universe' clones Okta, Slack, Jira, Google Workspace.
Generic SaaS twin; predates dark factories but underpins them.
Per-task container/VM spun up for an agent (Signadot, Northflank, Coder, e2b) — the substrate for harnesses.
Isolated execution environment in which an agent's actions are confined; required for safe dark-factory operation.
Datadog/Augment framing: harnesses driven by traces, metrics and prod telemetry, not just unit tests.
ArXiv proposal for self-evolving harnesses with component/experience/decision observability.
Stronger framing: code that fixes its own errors and rewrites its own tests; full dark factory is a weak form of RSI.
Marketing-friendly cousin: production code that detects and repairs itself, often at the edges of dark factories.
CI/CD adapted so agents (not humans) commit and merge.
Pattern in which agents only ship via PRs that pass CI; SWE-AF, OpenAI Codex Cloud, Devin all use it.
Factory.ai's branding for a per-task autonomous agent; effectively a dark-factory worker.
Specs versioned alongside code; a precondition for reproducible dark-factory runs.
Contrast with HITL; the dark factory pushes humans out of the inner loop entirely.
The org-chart side: small teams, no PRs to review, role of PM and SRE shifts.
StrongDM's controversial benchmark for whether a team is using its factory enough.
Per-step traces, costs, decisions; required for diagnosis when no human reads the code.
Policy layer that keeps agents inside guardrails; SWE-AF's governance agent is an example.

related term: Claude Code / Codex / Devin / Cursor Cloud
relevance to pattern: Concrete tools used to staff dark factories at the time of writing.
First-party big-lab survey of agentic coding adoption at frontier labs.
Benchmarks the dark-factory community uses to compare agents on real GitHub issues.
METR's 2026 measurement that agents can sustain ~14.5 hours of autonomous work, doubling every ~123 days.
Pejorative for low-quality AI output; dark factory critics worry it scales slop.
Pitfall: too many agents, lock contention, no ownership.
Argument that harnesses now matter more than model upgrades for end-to-end performance.
